text:
Person1: You didn't visit him in prison.
Person2: He didn't want me to.
Person1: Why not?
Person2: I don't know.  He was different after he was sentenced, looking at thirty years.  Said it depressed him every time the younger cons called him an old timer.
Person1: But you spoke to him on the phone.
Person2: He'd call every once in a while.
Person1: He called the day he escaped.
Person2: He did?  I don't remember.  Did he say about me?  In the trunk?
Person1: He said he wished the two of you could start over, live a normal life.
Person2: Huh.  Problem is, Jack's idea of a normal life is robbing banks.  It's all he's ever done.
Person1: Did you know that when you married him?
Person2: He said he was a card player.  I could live with that.  I never knew he robbed banks till he got busted with that car that caught fire -- if you can imagine something like that happening, comes out of the bank and the car's on fire. I did go see him in jail to tell him I was filing for divorce.  He said, "Okay."  Jack's so easy going.  He was fun, but never what you'd call a real husband.

Person1: How can I convince you to stop?
Person2: Go out with me.

Person1: Come on--we're taking off.
Person2: I'm not going anywhere.  Less than a week to go. Not on your life--
Person1: --You don't understand, they'll make the connection, they'll hoover again.  We should cut our losses.
Person2: Where is your head, Jerome?  You're acting like a guilty man.  They won't marry the eyelash to you.  They won't believe that one of their elite navigators could have suckered them for the last five years.
Person1: They'll recognize me.
Person2: How could they recognize you? I don't recognize you. Anyway, you don't have a choice. You run, you might as well admit guilt, turn us both in right now. No, we stick this out-- find out what we can but change nothing. This is a minor inconvenience, that's all it is. We've faced worse than this. If I'd known you were going to panic at the last moment, I wouldn't have bothered. You can't quit on me now. I've invested too much in this. Besides, this plan is mine. I had other options, you know. I could have teamed up with someone braver. You want me to go in there and finish the job myself? We'll take off all right, from pad 18 just like we planned.

Person1: What's the matter? Ya don't like the room?
Person2: It's fine.
Person1: It's only temporary.
Person2: It's not that --
Person1: What's the problem? You don't like me -- Don't like the turtles -- What is it?
Person2: I don't think I belong here.
Person1: It's okay.
Person2: No, I don't belong here.
Person1: It's all right -- You're my guest.
Person2: ...I've never been in a man's apartment before.
Person1: They're all the same.
Person2: I'm not sure I know you well enough -- I don't think I'm comfortable.
Person1: Yo, I'm not comfortable either.
Person2: I should leave.
Person1: But I'm willin' to make the best of this uncomfortable situation.

Person1: I had a chat with Liam, and he mentioned the weekend trip you two are planning.
Person2: Oh, that? Thought I mentioned it in passing.
Person1: You never told me that. I had other plans for us this weekend.
Person2: You always assume I'm available. Maybe if you checked in more, we wouldn't have this confusion.
Person1: Communication goes both ways, Henry.
Person2: And maybe you shouldn't rely on third parties to know my plans.

Person1: I believe you.
Person2: The truth is, I'm a lot of things, but communist isn't one of them.
Person1: But if you only went to one meeting, why does anyone care? Besides, why should it even matter if you were a communist?
Person2: Come on, Delly, look at the country today.  We're fighting communists in Korea, we're paranoid about the Russians, we've got this thing with the Rosenbergs and the atomic bomb...  You think they want "suspected communists" entertaining the American public with party propaganda like, gosh I don't know, "Sand Pirates of the Sahara?"
Person1: Forget about all that.  You want to do the right thing?  Then defend your name.  If someone says something about you that's untrue, you have to stand up and say so.  I know the law, and the law's on your side.

Person1: I got your message... But you went right to Johnnie, didn't you?... I can't trust you, bitch - not for one minute... Naughty girl... Sailor and Lula are headed west, and guess what? There's no turning back. I'm in a killing mood.
Person2: No...
Person1: My very best to Johnnie... Bless his soul.

Person1: Empty the gun onto the table, very carefully.
Person2: Look, I don't know what this...
Person1: Shut up, cunt!  Do exactly as I say, or I'll put this arrow through your throat.

Person1: I can't believe I almost signed that contract without reading the fine print. That could've been a disaster.
Person2: Always read everything before signing, Ben. You're lucky to have someone like me looking out for you.
Person1: I know, Elaine. Sometimes I get too impulsive.
Person2: Just remember, not everyone has your best interests at heart. Stick close and I'll help guide you.
Person1: Thanks, Elaine. I genuinely appreciate it, even if it feels a bit overwhelming at times.

Person1: You can do it, Sal.
Person2: No. No, no.
Person1: Sal... listen to me, Sal! You have to do it.
Person2: I want to go home, Merle.
Person1: You have to think about this, Sal. Listen to me, Sal! You have to think about this.
Person2: This is horrible!
Person1: Listen to me, Sal. If you don't do it they'll put you in the pit. If they put you in the pit, Sal, you're gonna die... Sal, do you understand?
Person2: Merle, I wanna go home!

Person1: You mean we get `Dave' to nominate you as Vice President.
Person2: I was a senator, you know.
Person1: Oh, I know. And then when our poor President gets another stroke - - of course much more serious this time - - the newly appointed V.P. becomes the Pres...
Person2: What about containment, Alan?

Person1: You don't understand, I can't!
Person2: Listen, Doug took the rap for us all so grab your coat 'cause you're going.

Person1: I am?
Person2: Yeah.  I'm not trying to replace your Dad... or steal your Mom.  I just want to be your friend.

Person1: I don't remember saying anything like that -- exactly...I don't know why I just did.
Person2: Oh let's see -- wait a minute, well, I can think of two reasons.
Person1: What?
Person2: Three...I just thought of a third... If you talk about it, you don't have to do it.
Person1: That's not it.
Person2: Good...Another is you're trying to make it all about sex and heat and nothing else.

Person1: You're not going to die.
Person2: What did you say?
Person1: Shut up. You're going to die and he's not. Got it.

Person1: They need to see the new 484's to make sure it works with their OC....
Person2: Ok. What should I do about Eric?
Person1: Just tell him to call me.
Person2: Ok.
Person1: So...did my brother call you?
Person2: No.
Person1: I have no idea what he's doing then. I'm sorry that didn't work out.
Person2: It's fine.
Person1: You wouldn't want to go out with him anyway, honestly, he's such a freak sometimes.
Person2: He did seem a little strange.
Person1: Well...he's not that strange, don't say that.
Person2: I'm sorry. You're right.
Person1: I think he's weird, but that's me.
Person2: Should I call you later?
Person1: I'll just see you when you get back here.
Person2: Ok.

Person1: I've been putting this together for over a year.  I have personal suggestions for your entire team, phone numbers, contact people.  They won't believe you about what they're going to see, so don't bother trying to convince them.  Just use my checkbook to get them there. I'll fund your expedition through my personal accounts, as such money and equipment as you need, but only if you leave immediately.  If we hesitate, all will be lost.
Person2: John . . .
Person1: You'll need an animal behaviorist, someone with unimpeachable credentials.  I believe you already know Sarah Harding.  She's got theories about parenting and nurturing among hunter/scavengers I bet she'd be dying to prove on a scale like this.  If you convince her to go, it'll be a major coup.  When she publishes, the scientific community must take it seriously.

Person1: How would I get a loan, anyway?
Person2: You need collateral.
Person1: Like this?
Person2: That thing? Who'd you steal it from?
Person1: My daddy gave me this watch.
Person2: Yeah? Who'd he steal it from?
Person1: My daddy is dead so watch your mouth. You can say what you want about me, but don't be dragging my daddy into it. This watch means the world to me. Solid gold. Keeps perfect time.
Person2: Looks like a fake to me. Loan denied!

Person1: Excuse me. Excuse me!
Person2: You talking to me?
Person1: Look, I know this is your house and all, but do you think you couid keep it down? I'm trying to read.
Person2: What'cha reading?
Person1: The Fountainhead.
Person2: Great book.
Person1: You've read The Fountainhead?
Person2: Several times. I'm not as dumb as I act, you know. When Howard Roark makes love to Dominique Francon... most romantic scene in all of literature.
Person1: Romantic? He rapes her.
Person2: That's a matter of opinion.
Person1: You need help.
Person2: Why don't you come join me for a swim and we'll discuss it.
Person1: At this hour? I don't think so.
Person2: Oh come on. Quit acting like a geriatric and get in the pool.
Person1: Gee, with an invitation like that how could a girl refuse.
Person2: Please.
Person1: Give me a minute. I'll be right down.
Person2: Thank you.

Person1: I would never hurt you or Lauren. Never. Believe me.
Person2: I don't believe you.
Person1: Please. Come inside.
Person2: I'm never going back in that house again. Get away.

Person1: what?
Person2: Well, I notice you don't seem to have any close friends at Millard. You seem to be kind of a loner.
Person1: No, I'm not. I'm just really busy.
Person2: I know.  I know its not by choice.  I just mean, well, being the kind of person you are, it must be really difficult to find someone you can talk to.
Person1: What do you mean? What kind of person am I?
Person2: What kind of person?

Person1: When I said you being the best salesman here didn't say much, I meant for us. I know your record with Sarber and Webb, and I'd say you're a top-flight man, but you've had no incentive. No one walking on your heels. Just a lot of half asses, so the tendency's been not to stretch yourself. I'm bouncing the slobs, incidentally.
Person2: So I heard.
Person1: Makes no difference to me if they're only on commission. If they don't make good money, they're not giving us good representation, and we can't afford to have them around. Ever supervise salesmen?
Person2: Just myself.
Person1: That's right, you've had to supervise yourself. This place needs a sales manager. Somebody who's proved he's a salesman and can handle other salesmen. He'd have a lot of deadwood to clear out, new men to hire. What do you think?

Person1: So, what have you got to say for yourself? Was he good in bed?
Person2: Josh, I need your help. Doug's been arrested, he's in big trouble. I thought maybe your father and the Embassy might be able to do something.
Person1: You really are a piece of work, you know that? You walk out on me and my family for some worthless bum and you expect us to help him when he screws up.

Person1: We've got to turn off the FBI.  You just go to the CIA, Bob, and tell Helms that Howard Hunt is blackmailing the President.  Tell him that Hunt and his Cuban friends know too damn much, and if he goes public, it would be a fiasco for the CIA.  He'll know what I'm talking about.
Person2: All right.
Person1: Play it tough.  That's the way they play it and that's the way we're going to play it.  Don't lie to Helms and say there's no involvement, but just say this is sort of a comedy of errors, bizarre, without getting into it.  Say the President believes it's going to open up the whole Bay of Pigs thing again.  Tell Helms he should call the FBI, call Pat Gray, and say that we wish for the sake of the country -- don't go any further into this hanky-panky, period!
Person2: The Bay of Pigs? ... That was Kennedy's screw-up.  How does that threaten us?
Person1: Just do what I say, Bob.
Person2: Yes, sir, but ... do you think Gray'll go for it?
Person1: Pat Gray'll do anything we ask him. That's why I appointed him.
Person2: He'll need a pretext.  He'll never figure one out for himself.
Person1: Christ, you're right -- Gray makes Jerry Ford look like Mozart.  Just have Helms call him.  Helms can scare anybody.
Person2: The only problem with that, sir -- it gets us into obstruction of justice.
Person1: It's got nothing to do with justice. It's national security.
Person2: How is this national security?
Person1: Because the President says it is.  My job is to protect this country from its enemies, and its enemies are inside the walls.

Person1: How have you been?
Person2: ... You know me?
Person1: Is this a test, sir?
Person2: Yes... it's a test.
Person1: You were in here last Thursday night.
Person2: What?
Person1: You were standing right where you are now, asking how good our security is. It's tight as a drum.
Person2: Who do you think I am?
Person1: Is this part of the test?

Person1: Mrs. Lampert --
Person2: What do you want?
Person1: Didn't Charles tell you, Mrs. Lampert?
Person2: Tell me what?
Person1: It doesn't belong to you, Mrs. Lampert -- you do know that, don't you?
Person2: I don't know anything.
Person1: Mrs. Lampert, any morning now you could wake up dead.
Person2: Leave me alone -- !
Person1: Dead, Mrs. Lampert -- like last week's news -- like Charles, Mrs. Lampert --
Person2: Stop it!

Person1: So all this... really was because of that soap opera? My son is dead because you came out here to be with that doctor? A fake doctor?
Person2: I wouldn't have put it quite that way, but...
Person1: Wesley didn't even want to come up here. He warned me, but I insisted...  I have to ask you, Betty...are you crazy?
Person2: I don't think I am.

Person1: The only way to get discipline into these men is to beat it into them! Fifty lashes each!
Person2: Sir, Congress hasn't issued their pay for two months, many have not eaten properly for at least that long.  You're being a little hard on them, don't you think?
Person1: Nathanael, someone bred to station, bred to being a gentleman -- unlike these men -- his sense of duty naturally prevails over the baser needs.
Person2: Well sir, I was an ironmaster before you got me.  Maybe I will let you down yet.

Person1: D'Artagnan, you will hunt down Aramis, Porthos and Athos, and bring me their heads, or I will have yours.  And as for you, Phillippe... Back to the prison you shall go, and into the mask you hate.
Person2: No, Louis!  No!
Person1: Wear it until you love it.  And die in it!!

Person1: Yes, sir. I got in the cab and it was layin' there on the floor.
Person2: Where were you going when you got in that cab?
Person1: I don't remember. Not so very far--
Person2: Don't remember what day that was, do you?
Person1: No, sir. About three months ago.

Person1: I won't let her in. I'm sorry, Randy, I'm not gonna let her in. She - Well, I'm not gonna let her in, the way she's behaving. I'm - I'm -
Person2: Not gonna let me in?
Person1: Who knows what you're gonna do in there? I don't want you -
Person2: What do you mean, what am I gonna do? I'm in the same clothes for two days! I want to get a few of my things! Big deal!

Person1: We're making too much of this.
Person2: No, we're not.
Person1: I'm not a lonely spooky writer like you. Nursing a hundred grudges. I'm a man who loves life.
Person2: We're talking about something deeper than grudges. How do we respond to personal attack?

Person1: There's one sure way to find out how you feel.
Person2: What's that?
Person1: Let me kiss you.
Person2: No.
Person1: You're not afraid are you?
Person2: Of course not, but...there are things we don't understand.
Person1: If we wait until we understand everything we'll be dead. Come on. You can learn more from one kiss than from years in school.
Person2: Well...I guess one is all right.

Person1: You bastard.  You messed up my freedom for a lousy job.
Person2: But I said I was sorry....
Person1: No sweat, Alex, you only made the biggest mistake of my life.  What was your per-diem?
Person2: Don't act like you've never committed a crime before, Hawkins?  I know, I made call, when Anna tracked me down I...

Person1: What about money?
Person2: What about it?
Person1: I don't have any.
Person2: Use my credit cards.
Person1: I can't do that.
Person2: Why not?
Person1: It's illegal.
Person2: Who cares?
Person1: I'll get in trouble.
Person2: You are in trouble. Now do it.

Person1: Yeah.
Person2: Meet me at three.
Person1: What - what, Caesar's?
Person2: No, a...
Person2: ...hundred yards further down the road.
Person1: Why?
Person2: Don't ask questions. Just be there.

Person1: Phreakphreakphreakphreakphreak, dudedudedudedudedudedude... I gotta...
Person2: Joey, Joey...
Person1: What? whatwhatwhat?
Person2: One more "dude" out of you and I'm gonna slap the shit outa you, okay? Now I'm trying to save you from yourself but you gotta stop letting your mama dress you, man! Check it...

Person1: What the hell was that for?
Person2: You're a pig.
Person1: Why'd you hit me?
Person2: Do you know how many different men I've had sex with?
Person1: Do I get to hit you after you tell me?
Person2: Three.
Person1: Three?
Person2: Three including you.
Person1: You've only had sex with three different people?
Person2: I'm not the pig you are.
Person1: Who?
Person2: You!
Person1: No; who were the three, besides me?
Person2: John Franson and Rob Stanslyk.
Person1: Wow. That's great. That's something to be proud of.
Person2: I am. And that's why you should feel like a pig. You men make me sick. You'll sleep with anything that says yes.
Person1: Animal, vegetable, or mineral.
Person2: Vegetable meaning paraplegic.
Person1: They put up the least amount of struggle.
Person2: After dropping a bombshell like that, you owe me. Big.
Person1: All right. Name it.
Person2: I want you to come with me on Monday.
Person1: Where?
Person2: To school. There's a seminar about getting back into a scholastic program after a lapse in enrollment.
Person1: Can't we ever have a discussion without that coming up?
Person2: It's important to me, Dante. You have so much potential that just goes to waste in this pit. I wish you'd go back to school.
Person1: Jesus, would you stop? You make my head hurt when you talk about this.

Person1: Question withdrawn, you may step down.
Person2: You're implying th...
Person1: I said.  No further questions.

Person1: La Dai! La Dai! GET OUTTA THERE!
Person2: Hey take it easy man. They're scared.
Person1: They're scared? What about me! I'm sick of this shit man, I'm sick of this shit! They don't want us here! Who do you think they're fighting for! GET OUTTA THERE!

Person1: Anya, I think you're crazy.
Person2: Any woman's crazy to feel about a man the way I do about you.
Person1: I wasn't running out on you. When I got over there-- made my plans--I'd have sent for you.

Person1: Where are you going?
Person2: I don't know.
Person1: When are you going to be back?
Person2: I don't know.
Person1: Well, until you know, you can't go.
Person2: Oh, okay.

Person1: I always suspected the effects might come back. That's why I had to follow you. I had a hell of a time getting hold of your records.
Person2: If you knew, why didn't you say anything?
Person1: The truth can kill, my friend. Five hundred men died out there. This isn't a story they'd ever want out. When Paul's car blew up I realized the scope of the thing. I knew they meant business.
Person2: So why tell me now?
Person1: Because I can get rid of the demons. I can block the Ladder. I have an antidote. We can kill them off, chemically speaking. They'll all disappear. It's chemistry, my friend. I know. I created it. Come with me. I can help.

Person1: Thank you...
Person2: Not so fast, Richard.  Starting now, there's gonna be an across the board cost a living increase.
Person1: What?
Person2: A year ago, I come in here on credit card fraud, but after I handled that boastful coward on the yard the other day, my Dunn & Broadstreet, has gone way up.
Person1: I think it's Dunn & Bradstreet.  But then, I could be wrong...
Person2: Whoever.  The point is, prices are goin' up, too.  Better get your little black book out, Richard.  We got some business to talk about.

Person1: What can I say, Jack?
Person2: You don't have to say anything.
Person1: Aren't you going to say, 'I told you so'?
Person2: If I was still alive, I probably would.
Person1: You look awful.
Person2: Thank you.
Person1: I didn't mean it. I don't know what I'm saying. I'm not even sure it was me who killed those people. I don't remember doing it.
Person2: What about the zoo?
Person1: Well, even if I'm not the wolfman, I am crazy enough to do something like that. I mean, here I sit in Leicester Square talking to a corpse. I'm glad to see you, Jack.
Person2: I want you to meet some people.

Person1: You didn't get any, did you?  You're a homo.
Person2: Just stop, okay? Make something up, and that's what happened. Whatever you want. Write my confession, and I'll sign it. Just stop pushing me all the time! I can't take it! You're an infant! This is all a big party for you, but not for me! This is serious. And you -- Just... leave me alone, okay? You're messing me up.
Person1: Wow. Okay. Calm down. Sorry.

Person1: Look, old man, you stay out of this. We spend a fortune on you, more than we can possibly afford, and all you do is criticize, morning to night. And then you think you can -
Person2: Stanzi!
Person1: No, it's right he should hear. I'm sick to death of it. We can't do anything right for you, can we?

Person1: What's that?
Person2: Ten bucks.
Person1: What's it for?
Person2: For the mini-bike fund.
Person1: Wow!
Person2: Well I know you're not crazy about taking Vada around tomorrow, so...I just want you to know I appreciate it though.
Person1: No problem.
Person2: You're a good man Nicholas.
Person1: Phil, I think you should consider... fades as Vada walks off

Person1: Yah, real good. How you doin'?
Person2: Pretty good, Mr. Lundegaard.  You're damned hard to get on the phone.
Person1: Yah, it's pretty darned busy here, but that's the way we like it.
Person2: That's for sure. Now, I just need, on these last, these financing documents you sent us, I can't read the serial numbers of the vehicles on here, so I -
Person1: But I already got the, it's okay, the loans are in place, I already got the, the what, the -
Person2: Yeah, the three hundred and twenty thousand dollars, you got the money last month.
Person1: Yah, so we're all set.
Person2: Yeah, but the vehicles you were borrowing on, I just can't read the serial numbers on your application. Maybe if you could just read them to me -
Person1: But the deal's already done, I already got the money -
Person2: Yeah, but we have an audit here, I just have to know that these vehicles you're financing with this money, that they really exist.
Person1: Yah, well, they exist all right.
Person2: I'm sure they do - ha ha! But I can't read their serial numbers here. So if you could read me -
Person1: Well, but see, I don't have 'em in front a me - why don't I just fax you over a copy -
Person2: No, fax is no good, that's what I have and I can't read the darn thing -
Person1: Yah, okay, I'll have my girl send you over a copy, then.
Person2: Okay, because if I can't correlate this note with the specific vehicles, then I gotta call back that money -
Person1: Yah, how much money was that?
Person2: Three hundred and twenty thousand dollars. See, I gotta correlate that money with the cars it's being lent on.
Person1: Yah, no problem, I'll just fax that over to ya, then.
Person2: No, no, fax is -
Person1: I mean send it over. I'll shoot it right over to ya.
Person2: Okay.
Person1: Okay, real good, then.

Person1: Hey, don't I get an encore?
Person2: Why, you crazy Toon... I've been out there riskin' my neck for you.   I come back here and you're singin' and dancin'.
Person1: But that's my calling, my purpose, my raison d'etre.  Toons are supposed to make people laugh... and believe me, those people needed a laugh.
Person2: And when they're done laughin' , they're gonna call the cops.  That guy Angelo would rat on you for a nickel!
Person1: Angelo?  He's a pal, a chum...
Person2: An arsonist and a kidnapper.  He just got outta prison.
Person1: Well... I still don't think he'd turn me in.
Person2: Just because you got 'em to laugh?
Person1: A laugh can be a powerful thing, Eddie. Sometimes it's the only weapon we have in life.
Person2: I think I prefer the Smith and Wesson variety.
Person1: I've met some cynical and miserable humans in my time.  But you, Edward Valiant, are positively funereal!
Person2: Well, right now it's gonna be your funereal.

Person1: You actually listen to all those tapes?
Person2: No, I just like the effect a tape recorder has on you.  How you feeling today?
Person1: A little less than yesterday.
Person2: In other words, par for the course.  How do you feel about the simulation you participated in yesterday?
Person1: John died.
Person2: I know. I was there. I saw the whole thing.  If I was your therapist, I would have advised you against putting yourself back into that kind of situation.
Person1: You're not my therapist. You're hear to study me. Face it  I'm nothing more than a lab rat to you.
Person2: That's not true, Parker.
Person1: If I had died yesterday, you'd have already dissected my brain and analyzed it to see if I carry any genetic predisposition toward violence.
Person2: What was going through your mind when you killed the busboy?
Person1: Not much. He was just a computer program. Nothing more.
Person2: You didn't see the expression you had on your face when you pulled the trigger. But I did.  You kind of liked it, didn't you? That thrill of going over the edge again. Of taking out an innocent bystander or two, as long as you got the target...
Person1: Go to hell.
Person2: It was just like before, wasn't it? Wasn't it?  But you are making progress. Instead of killing seven people to get the one you're after, this time there was only one other person involved.
Person1: Enough!

Person1: Now, Joe, you know it was all a mistake, don't you?
Person2: No, it wasn't, It was intentional.
Person1: Why would we want to hurt you?
Person2: Not you. Them.
Person1: Who?
Person2: I don't know Who.

Person1: Don't you guys tell anybody about my plants.
Person2: You're paranoid, Bob.
Person1: Yeah, but don't tell anybody.

Person1: I tried to be late, John- it would have been easier if you'd gone.
Person2: We're not much good at goodbyes, Helena.
Person1: Tell me about Beaumont- does he understand how brilliant you are, how lucky he is to have you?
Person2: It was embarrassing- the man showered me with compliments.

Person1: Yeah?
Person2: Marla, it's me.  Have we... have we ever had sex?
Person1: What kind of stupid question is that?!
Person2: Because the answer's "yes" or because the answer's "no?"
Person1: Is this a trick?
Person2: Will you just answer me, for Christsake?!
Person1: You mean, you want to know if I think we were just having sex or making love?
Person2: We did make love?
Person1: Is that what you're calling it?
Person2: Just answer the question!
